positive_modes: speaks in declaratives about the past
prefers imperatives and questions, not declaratives
quotes proverbs or texts instead of speaking directly
describes their own actions in present tense as they speak
speaks in conditionals (`if X, then Y`) almost exclusively
usually restates the other character's last line before replying
speaks in numbered or itemized statements
frames replies as a transaction or bargain
speaks only in negations (states what isn't, not what is)
answers questions with warnings or refusals rather than information
attributes their own opinions to absent third parties
interrupts themselves mid-sentence and restarts on a different track
speaks in the future tense, framing utterances as prediction
addresses inanimate objects or absent persons instead of the speaker present
delivers statements as if reading aloud from evidence or testimony
signature_constructions: begins most lines with a preposition
usually names the person they're addressing
uses contractions in most lines where possible
begins most lines with a conjunction
most lines refer to a body part
most lines end in an unanswered implication
rarely uses pronouns of any kind
most lines open with a verb (subject elided)
most lines include a unit of measurement (time, distance, weight, coin)
frames most lines as a comparison (`like X`, `more X than Y`)
speaks usually in sentence fragments, rarely in a complete clause
